channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Ligue 1: Lille qualify for Champions League despite last matchday loss
item.description: Lille lost to Auxerre (2-0) but retain third place at the end of the final matchday of Ligue 1. Lyon finish fourth and Marseille fifth.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: France's justice minister to visit Algeria amid diplomatic thaw
item.description: France's Justice Minister Gérald Darmanin is set to visit Algeria on Monday amid a diplomatic thaw to discuss improving judicial cooperation, including the fate of detained French sports journalist Christophe Gleizes, who was arrested in 2024 for "glorifying terrorism".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East war live: Drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia fuel escalation fears
item.description: A drone strike caused a fire at a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, officials there said on Sunday, while Saudi Arabia reported intercepting three drones, as US President Donald Trump warned that Iran must act "fast" after efforts to end the US-Israeli war with Iran appeared to have stalled. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.source: © Arun Girija, Emirates News Agency via AP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: California jury to deliberate on Musk's blockbuster lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: A jury is set to deliberate Monday on Silicon Valley's first major AI trial, pitting billionaire Elon Musk against AI giant OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman. In his lawsuit, Musk argued that OpenAI's pivot to an $850 billion juggernaut betrayed its original nonprofit mandate and that it improperly used a $38 million donation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius arrives in Rotterdam for disinfection
item.description: The hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius on Monday reached the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection as authorities prepared quarantine arrangements for remaining crew and medical staff following an outbreak that has killed three people and triggered global monitoring.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump warns Iran that 'there won't be anything left of them' without peace deal
item.description: US President Donald Trump warned Iran on Sunday to move quickly towards a peace deal or face destruction, as Washington’s war with Tehran and its allies continues to stall negotiations, drive up energy prices and fuel instability across the Middle East.
item.source: © Evan Vucci, Pool Reuters via AP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 'Staggering' Iran toll drives up global executions to 40-year high, Amnesty says
item.description: The number of worldwide executions jumped to their highest level since 1981 in 2025, pushed in part by a "staggering" increase in Iran's use of the death penalty, according to Amnesty International on Monday. The UK-based rights group said it had confirmed the executions of at least 2,707 people globally, 2,159 of which were in Iran.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Julianne Moore says true gender equality still distant at Cannes
item.description: At a Women in Motion event held alongside the Cannes Film Festival on Sunday, Oscar-winning actor Julianne Moore said true gender equality remains far off globally and in the film industry as she accepted an award recognising her advocacy for women in cinema.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Drone strike sparks a fire on the edge of UAE nuclear facility
item.description: A drone strike sparked a fire near the United Arab Emirates’s Barakah nuclear power plant on Sunday, authorities said, in an attack Abu Dhabi condemned as a threat to regional security. The strike highlighted the possibility of renewed war amid a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran.
item.source: © Handout picture from the media office of the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant via AFP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Suspected Ukrainian military drone discovered after crashing in Lithuania
item.description: A suspected Ukrainian military drone was found crashed in Lithuania on Sunday, officials said, as Baltic NATO members face growing concern over stray drones crossing into their airspace from the war in neighboring Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: England defeat France to secure eighth consecutive Women’s Six Nations title
item.description: England won a record eighth consecutive Women's Six Nations title and fifth successive Grand Slam after beating host France 43-28 on Sunday. Unbeaten France, with backing from its largest ever home crowd, was expected to seriously threaten England's relentless domination of the championship.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Cannes Film Festival: Follow the latest news with Eve Jackson
item.description: Eve Jackson is live from Cannes.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Bolivian police clash with protesters blocking roads into La Paz
item.description: Bolivian police and soldiers clashed Saturday with protesters blocking roads into La Paz to press for wage increases and other demands.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France’s film giant Canal+ to blacklist hundreds over petition against right-wing owner
item.description: France's biggest film producer Canal+ will no longer work with hundreds of industry professionals who signed a petition against right-wing owner Vincent Bolloré, the group's chief executive announced Sunday at the Cannes Film Festival, sending shockwaves through the European film industry. The petition called on people to mobilise against "the growing grip of the far right" on French film.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Most of the architects of October 7 attacks have been killed, says Netanyahu
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Sunday that Israel is close to finishing a key war aim in Gaza: killing the planners of the October 7 attacks. The comments came after Israel said it had killed senior Hamas commander Ezzedine Al-Haddad in an airstrike, as its campaign against the group’s leadership continues.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russia: Zelensky says response to Moscow's agression 'justified'
item.description: Douglas Herbert has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Drone hits UAE nuclear power plant: IAEA reports normal radiation levels
item.description: Hoda Abdel-Hamid is live from Doha, Qatar.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: WHO declares emergency as Ebola outbreak kills more than 80 in DR Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization declared an international health emergency on Sunday over an outbreak of an Ebola strain in the Democratic Republic of Congo that has killed more than 80 and for which there is no vaccine.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ebola outbreak declared health emergency in DR Congo and Uganda
item.description: Ariel Kestens, Head of IFRC Kinshasa Delegation, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: "One Nation under God": US President Trump joins national prayer rally
item.description: André Gagné, Full Professor and Chair, Concordia University of Montreal, Canada, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia kills at least 4 and wound 12 others
item.description: One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia killed at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounded a dozen others, local authorities said Sunday. Debris fell on Russia's largest airport without causing damage.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ebola: WHO warns about risk of spread in DR Congo and Uganda
item.description: Trish Newport, Emergency Programme Manager for MSF's Ebola response in Ituri, DRC, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Are Google and SpaceX plotting space-based data centres?
item.description: Tech giants are increasingly looking beyond Earth to solve one of AI's biggest growing pains: where to put all the computers.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Lam

item.title: UK leadership crisis: 'Starmer is a lame duck prime minister,' says former Labour adviser
item.description: It’s been a tumultuous week for Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who is facing a leadership crisis. Following a chaotic week in which nearly 90 Labour MPs called on Starmer to resign, along with a series of ministerial resignations, the prime minister is clinging on to power.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE
